description: Modify an existing entry in the CMAT (Contact Model Assignment Table). Assigns the information specified by the keywords to the entry at index i of the CMAT. This command allows updating contact model, properties, methods, and other settings for an existing CMAT entry without removing and re-adding it. The range specification is optional. By default, only mechanical contacts are considered.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.command('contact cmat modify ...') - Python SDK does not provide direct CMAT API, must use command interface

model_properties_reference.description: For detailed property information for each contact model, see the model-properties reference documentation

notes: Entry index i must be a valid existing entry (use 'contact cmat list' to check)
Index 0 is the default entry and can be modified
Only specified keywords are updated - unspecified properties retain their previous values
Unlike 'remove and add', modify preserves the entry index
Modification only affects how new contacts are assigned
To update existing contacts after modifying CMAT, use 'contact cmat apply'
Multiple properties can be set in a single command
Multiple methods can be registered in a single command
Range specification is optional - if omitted, previous range is retained

common_use_cases.command: contact cmat modify 1 property pb_ten 2e8 pb_coh 1.5e8
common_use_cases.description: Adjust strength parameters based on calibration results without changing model or stiffnesses

common_use_cases.use_case: Change contact model for existing entry
common_use_cases.command: contact cmat modify 2 model hertz property hz_shear 2e9 hz_poiss 0.25 fric 0.5
common_use_cases.description: Switch from one model to another and set new model's properties

common_use_cases.command: contact cmat modify 3 property dp_nratio 0.7 dp_sratio 0.5 dp_mode 1
common_use_cases.description: Add dashpot damping to an existing entry for quasi-static simulations

common_use_cases.command: contact cmat modify 0 model linear property kn 1e9 ks 5e8 fric 0.5
common_use_cases.description: Change the default contact model and properties

common_use_cases.command: contact cmat modify 1 model linearpbond method deformability emod 5e9 kratio 1.5 method pb_deformability emod 5e10 kratio 1.5 property pb_ten 1e7 pb_coh 5e6 pb_fa 35.0
common_use_cases.description: Comprehensive modification including model, methods, and properties

typical_workflow: 1. Use 'contact cmat list' to identify the entry index to modify
2. Execute 'contact cmat modify <i>' with desired changes
4. Note that only new contacts will use the modified entry
5. If needed, use 'contact cmat apply' to update existing contacts
6. Test contact creation to ensure modified entry works as expected

modify_vs_add.modify: Updates existing entry at specific index, preserves index number
modify_vs_add.add: Creates new entry, may insert at specified index or add to end

modify_vs_remove.modify: Changes entry content, entry remains in CMAT
modify_vs_remove.remove: Deletes entry completely, subsequent entries shift down

modify_vs_default.modify: Can modify any entry including default (index 0), requires explicit index
modify_vs_default.default: Specifically for setting default entry, can use 'type' keyword to target specific contact types

partial_modification_behavior.description: When modifying an entry, only the specified keywords are updated. Unspecified properties retain their previous values.

examples.scenario: Entry has model=linear, kn=1e8, ks=5e7, fric=0.5
examples.command: contact cmat modify 1 property fric 0.6
examples.result: Entry now has model=linear, kn=1e8, ks=5e7, fric=0.6 (only fric updated)

examples.command: contact cmat modify 1 model linearpbond property pb_ten 1e7
examples.result: Model changed to linearpbond, pb_ten set, but previous linear properties are lost (model change is comprehensive)

warnings.message: Changing the model keyword replaces the entire contact model - previous model-specific properties are lost
warnings.mitigation: When changing models, specify all required properties for the new model

warnings.message: Modifying CMAT entries does NOT affect existing contacts automatically
warnings.mitigation: Use 'contact cmat apply' after modification to update existing contacts

troubleshooting.solution: Use 'contact cmat list' to verify entry exists at that index
Modification only affects new contacts. Use 'contact cmat apply' to update existing contacts

troubleshooting.issue: Changed model but properties seem wrong
troubleshooting.solution: When changing models, all model-specific properties should be re-specified as previous model's properties are lost

keywords.name: method
keywords.description: The contact model method s , along with its specific list of pairs of argument names and values ( sa , a ) (if applicable), is registered with this cmat entry and will be invoked at contact creation.

keywords.name: softbond
keywords.description: Specify the contact model. The list of built-in mechanical contact models is given in the “ Built-in Contact Models ” section. Users may also implement custom contact models (see “ User-Defined Contact Models ” for further information) and register them in the CMAT. Specify the Adhesive Rolling Resistance Linear Model contact model. Specify the Bilinear Contact Model contact model. Specify the Burger’s Model contact model. Specify the FISH Model contact model. Specify the Flat-Joint Model contact model. Specify the Hertz Model contact model. Specify the Hysteretic Model contact model. Specify the Linear Model contact model. Specify the Linear Contact Bond Model contact model. Specify the Linear Dipole Model contact model. Specify the Linear Parallel Bond Model contact model. Specify the Null Model contact model. Specify the Rolling Resistance Linear Model contact model. Specify the Smooth-Joint Model contact model. Specify the Soft-Bond Model contact model.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: The contact model property named sp is set to a .

keywords.name: proximity
keywords.description: The detection distance is set to fd .

examples.command: contact cmat modify 1 model linearpbond method deformability emod 1e6 kratio 0.5 method pb_deformability emod 1e6 kratio 0.5 property fric 0.25 dp_nratio 0.2 pb_ten 1e8 pb_coh 1e8 pb_fa 30.0
examples.description: Modify entry 1 to use linearpbond model with deformability methods and specified properties (from official example)

examples.command: contact cmat modify 2 model linear property kn 1e8 ks 5e7 fric 0.5
examples.description: Change entry 2 to use linear model with updated stiffness and friction

examples.command: contact cmat modify 3 property pb_ten 2e8 pb_coh 1e8
examples.description: Update only the strength properties of entry 3 without changing model or other properties

examples.command: contact cmat modify 0 model linear property kn 1e8 ks 5e7 fric 0.5
examples.description: Modify the default entry (index 0) to change the fallback contact model

examples.command: contact cmat modify 1 property fric 0.6
examples.description: Update only the friction coefficient for entry 1

examples.command: contact cmat modify 2 range group 'soil'
examples.description: Update the range specification for entry 2 to apply to 'soil' group

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: The contact model property named sp is set to a . The list of available properties is contact model specific. Visit the documentation of the desired contact model for details.

keywords.name: range
keywords.description: Modify the range corresponding to this CMAT entry. This keyword must be followed by a valid range .
